text.title: business-model-research Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for business-model-research as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Building a comp set with only public-listed companies
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Survivorship bias: public companies run higher gross margin (78-82%) than venture-stage privates (60-70%) because of scale. Transplanting the median onto a $2M ARR target produces unachievable targets.

failure-mode.description: Freemium conversion cited as "20%" without denominator disclosure
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Companies cite conversion against activated or "qualified" users; system-wide conversion from signups is 1-4%. Always normalize to signups or the comparison is meaningless.

failure-mode.description: NDR reported without gross logo retention
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: NDR above 110% coexists with gross logo churn above 25% in land-and-expand; the combination indicates an expansion-dependent model at high risk if new logo growth slows.

failure-mode.description: Averaging cross-stage comps (seed + listed) without cohort annotation
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A seed-stage Crunchbase pricing page averaged with a listed 10-K produces metrics that apply to neither stage. Cohort by stage before computing any statistic.

failure-mode.description: Agent reads "annualized recurring revenue" and treats it as ARPU
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ARR / ARPU / ACV are three different denominators. Require a glossary block in the output before any metric. Conflating them produces LTV calculations that are 10-500x off.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Shopify revenue is approximately 40% subscription, 40% payments (Merchant Solutions), and 20% other. Collapsing it to subscription distorts the subscription archetype's gross margin median by 5+ points.

failure-mode.description: Agent reports marketplace headline commission (e.g., Etsy 6.5%) as the take rate
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: All-in take rate including seller subscriptions, payment fees, and ad revenue is 13-18% — 2x or more the headline commission. Headline commission misleads strategic pricing decisions.

failure-mode.description: Agent uses today's pricing page against last year's filing metrics
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SaaS pricing changes frequently; Wayback Machine snapshots at filing date are required to compare pricing to the financial metrics reported in the same period.

failure-mode.description: Agent ships a verdict ("this model is viable") instead of a distribution
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The market-researcher deliverable is distributions (P25/P50/P75). The verdict belongs to the researcher sibling and the product manager. Inverting this boundary mixes two distinct roles.
